def: The full right of a state to govern itself.
The division of government into law-making, executive, and judicial parts.
The work of planting and caring for a garden.
The activity of riding a bicycle.
The activity of catching fish with a rod and line.
The act of making music with the voice.
Making small things by hand as a hobby.
The craft of making pots and objects from clay.
The activity of playing or performing music.
To rest and let go of tension.
To calm and focus the mind through quiet reflection.
To test something to see if it works or suits you.
Helping you to rest and feel calm.
Showing strong, deep feeling or enthusiasm.
To spend time outdoors in the natural world.
A fitness expert who coaches one person's exercise.
A firm foam cylinder used to stretch and massage muscles.
Recording exercise and health data, often with a device.
Nutrients in foods like bread and pasta that give energy.
A machine with a moving belt for walking or running indoors.
The salty liquid the skin gives off when hot or exercising.
The number of times the heart beats per minute.
The beating of the heart as felt in the arteries.
An exercise that stretches the muscles to keep them supple.
Happening again and again at even intervals.
A picture made with paint.
A formal style of dance that tells a story through movement.
A long written story about invented characters and events.
A public show of art or objects for people to look at.
A person who creates art, such as paintings or music.
The traditions and works passed down from past generations.
A large group of musicians playing together.
A piece of writing in verse, often with rhythm or rhyme.
Good at making new things or having original ideas.
Relating to art, or showing skill in art.
Relating to the customs and arts of a society.
A ticket that lets you enter an event or place.
A close, friendly relationship between people.
Unhappy feelings caused by fear of losing someone's love.
The act of becoming friendly again after a quarrel.
Having promised to marry someone.
A woman one shares life or a relationship with.
A man you know slightly but who is not a close friend.
A woman you know slightly but who is not a close friend.
To look after someone or deal with something.
To make peace with someone after a disagreement.
To behave playfully to show romantic interest.
Showing politeness and regard for others.
To be able to rely on someone for help or support.
A member of an army.
A time without war or conflict; calm.
An area of land under the control of a ruler or state.
A single complete thing; also a group within an army.
A group that moves around an area to guard or watch it.
Carrying weapons.
A game played by moving pieces on a marked board.
A game played with a deck of cards.
A game played on a computer or console screen.
The number of points each side has in a game.
A competition made up of a series of games or rounds.
The person or team that wins a contest.
To pretend to be stronger or more sure than you really are.
Difficult in a way that tests your ability.
One of the small pieces that fit together to form a picture.
The sport of moving over snow on skis.
A single wide board for riding down snowy slopes.
A machine that carries skiers up a slope.
Protective glasses worn while skiing.
A person who teaches others how to ski.
A row of hanging seats that carry people up a mountain.
A mass of snow that slides suddenly down a mountain.
The social fun and drinks enjoyed after a day of skiing.
A small mountain building where skiers rest and eat.
Rising or falling sharply, at a big angle.
Covered with a layer of ice.
Fresh, soft, dry snow that is light and loose.
How deep the layer of snow is.
The act of coming to live permanently in another country.
Following the length or side of something.
A long, distinct period of history.
A protected area where wildlife and plants are preserved.
A plan or aim to do something.
The period of a person's life when they are a child.
Making something smaller in size, amount, or degree.
Communication or connection with a person.
A difficult matter that needs to be solved.
The way in which two things are not the same.
The effect or feeling that something leaves on you.
An idea formed in the mind.
Something you must do because it is right or required.
Hard physical or mental work put into something.
To make something able to happen.
To look after someone or deal with something.
Belonging to or concerning one particular person.
Used to introduce a fact that contrasts with the main point.
Used to introduce the first of two contrasting sides.
As a result of; owing to.
As an instance; for example.
By now; before this point in time.
Up to the present moment.
The national government of a federal country.
In favour of it; also in return for it.
In opposition to it; also by contrast.
For that reason; also from that place.
Away from it, or concerning it.
In addition; also for that purpose.
In the same way; too.
About or roughly; also 'for instance'.
A town or district with its own local government.
All the people born around the same time.
Support given to make something easier.
How high something is; also an amount.
Especially; more than others.
Taking everything together; altogether.
At any time; also 'each' or 'per'.
Used to introduce a contrast; nevertheless.
In short supply; barely enough or barely making it.
In the time since; by now.
Pushed through a sieve; strained until smooth.
By means of a particular method.
A structure around a picture; also the limits of something.
A large area of a country or the world.
About or roughly; also round in shape.
And also; together with.
Used with 'als auch' to mean 'both ... and'.
In place of something else.
The person who commits a crime.
In every way; completely.
If nothing more; as a minimum.
At the beginning; before anything else.
